decisions.summary: Scope reviewability accepted: this PR is one coherent readiness-projection change across CLI depth selection, PR gate command metadata, bounded projection preservation, focused tests, story/spec/architecture docs, and Design SSOT lineage registration.
decisions.reason: The runtime changes are localized to src/cli.js, src/pr-manager.js, and src/canonical-audit.js; tests directly bind the lightweight focused-view default, explicit-depth override, primary_next_command/next_commands projection, and overall-status fallback. Splitting would create artificial coupling between CLI output shape and gate metadata preservation.

decisions.summary: Scope reviewability decision: the PR remains a single reviewable story.
decisions.reason: alternatives_considered: split lightweight evidence-depth defaulting and next-command projection into separate PRs, but rejected because the user-facing problem is one PR readiness handoff loop and both changes are exercised by the same pr prepare surface. compatibility_impact: review surface remains small and localized to src/cli.js, src/pr-manager.js, src/canonical-audit.js, two focused tests, and story/spec docs. rollback_plan: revert the single commit and regenerate pr prepare artifacts if review finds the scope too broad. boundary: one story, one spec, one runtime surface, no unrelated refactor, no new product feature outside focused PR readiness views. accepted_followups: none. decision_record: accepted by codex for PR scope.

decisions.summary: Execution topology decision: no new runtime topology is introduced by the readiness projection change.
decisions.reason: alternatives_considered: move projection building into a new readiness command, but rejected because existing pr prepare already owns Gate DAG and PR readiness artifacts; keep full-depth evidence for all views, but rejected because it makes machine handoff views unnecessarily heavy. compatibility_impact: existing pr prepare lifecycle remains the same; durable artifacts, verification evidence, review artifacts, and explicit --evidence-depth behavior are unchanged. rollback_plan: revert the three source files and focused tests, then regenerate pr prepare artifacts against the previous behavior. boundary: no new worker, agent, subprocess, network call, persistence store, retry loop, or artifact lifecycle is added; the change only selects summary-depth projection for focused outputs and carries command-shaped hints. accepted_followups: none. topology_diagram: user invokes pr prepare -> CLI resolves focused view -> evidence depth defaults to summary unless explicit -> pr-manager builds Gate DAG and next commands -> canonical audit emits bounded projection -> durable pr-prepare artifact remains available for full drill-down.

decisions.summary: Public contract architecture decision: focused PR readiness view contract remains additive and backward compatible.
decisions.reason: alternatives_considered: keep full evidence projection for every pr prepare output, but rejected because focused views exist for machine handoff and do not need full durable evidence by default; add a separate command, but rejected because it would fragment existing pr prepare workflows. compatibility_impact: backward compatible for CLI consumers because explicit --evidence-depth still wins and full durable artifacts remain available from pr-prepare.json; the new next_commands and primary_next_command fields are additive. rollback_plan: revert src/cli.js, src/pr-manager.js, src/canonical-audit.js and the focused tests, then rerun pr prepare evidence. boundary: only bounded pr prepare --summary-json and --view projections receive the lighter default and command metadata; durable Gate DAG evaluation and full-depth override are unchanged. accepted_followups: none.

decisions.summary: ADR-unnecessary decision: focused PR readiness views stay within existing pr prepare, Gate DAG, and bounded projection architecture.
decisions.reason: The change only adjusts evidence-depth defaulting for bounded outputs and preserves durable Gate DAG evaluation plus explicit full-depth override; no new component, persistence model, or authority boundary is introduced.
